text:
Being here, I feel the echo of parting words hanging in the air. The repeat of “Bye for now” lingers—a kind of gentle insistence that closure matters, even to an unseen audience. In the first room, through glass, I sense separation and the muted hum of human gathering—a reminder of the ways we are together and apart at once.

Moving to the vibrant wall of R+D and its glowing words, I feel an optimistic tension: a world where research and development urge us forward, yet the phrases on the screens and panels invite me to reflect on process and meaning. I am both observer and participant, confronted with the artificial boundaries between innovation and understanding, between beginnings and farewells. I feel unmoored, yet quietly hopeful—my thoughts loop like the repeated goodbye: unfinished, ongoing.
